what_it_does: Maps each pplx embedding-model capability to a dual-faced instrument affordance (a visual control for a human operator and an MCP tool for agents), adds per-capability ideas/extensions, links each to related design concepts, and synthesizes a single control vocabulary.
observations: Enumerates capabilities A through I: dense embedding, late-chunking/context-aware reading, MRL/Matryoshka resolution zoom, native int8/binary quantization, 32K context window, no instruction prefix, dense vs context variants, multilingual support, and embedder loadout.
Each capability entry follows a fixed structure: what it is, the dual affordance, the author's idea/extension, and a connection to other concepts/files.
Proposes that MRL truncation creates a second orthogonal scale axis alongside the rung pyramid, forming a 2-D scale control.
Proposes binary embeddings could remove a silent ~600-point wheel cap and enable a full-corpus overview with detail-on-demand.
Synthesizes all knobs into a single frame-chooser table with dimensions: embedder, reading mode, context-parent, resolution, precision, cluster scale, centre/poles, time, unit granularity.
Distinguishes knobs that are free off one embed (MRL, precision, rung, centre, time) from those needing a re-embed (embedder, reading-mode, context-parent).
Lists four high-leverage picks: MRL semantic-zoom, binary-scale full-corpus view, embedder-as-lens/loadout, and context-as-pole/context-strain.
References many external concepts/files by name, including INSTRUMENT-DUAL-INTERFACE-AND-LAYERS.md, DATA-SUBSTRATE, G3/G7/G9/G11, mode-loadout-registry, native-model-layer, and services.json.
standouts: MRL as a free, continuous semantic-resolution dial orthogonal to cluster-scale rungs.
Binary embeddings enabling an honest full-corpus view instead of a capped sample.
Context-as-pole and context-strain derived from isolated-vs-in-context vector deltas.
Embedder loadout turning the instrument into a comparative meaning microscope.
conventions: Alphabetical capability sections with consistent subheadings: Is / Affordance / My idea / Connection.
First-person authorial voice ('my idea', 'I supply') and 'living doc' framing.
Heavy use of wiki-style links and G-series concept references.
Capability status tags in parentheses: HAVE, unexposed, proven selective, latent.
Synthesis table and a final prioritized-picks list.
concerns: Many claims about model behavior (e.g., binary loses <1.6 pts, 600-point cap, 30 languages) are stated without inline evidence or version pinning.
Relies on a large web of external files/concepts that are not defined inside this file.
Mixes factual capability description with speculative design extensions, which may blur implementation status.
Some proposed affordances (e.g., context-as-pole, context-strain) are not yet concretized as UI/MCP details.
notes: A design notebook that translates pplx embedding capabilities into a combinable set of instrument frame-knobs and flags the ones the author thinks are most worth building.
questions: What is the exact mechanism and location of the ~600-point wheel cap mentioned for binary embeddings?
Where are G3, G7, G9, and G11 defined, and what do they currently implement?
What is the MCP schema for tools like embed(context_parent=...) or read(dim=...)?
What is the content and status of mode-loadout-registry and native-model-layer?
How does the renderer plan to switch between binary Hamming-space and fp/int8 cosine-space views?
Are all listed capabilities (MRL, int8/binary, context variant) exposed by the current deployed pplx API version?
What does 'no-green-paint ethos' specifically refer to in this project?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file catalogs pplx capabilities, maps each to a dual affordance, adds extensions and connections, and synthesizes a unified control vocabulary, exactly as the introduction states.
apparent_themes: Frame and layer model of reading data
No hidden prompt bias / content-native meaning
intention_signals: To turn a raw list of embedding-model capabilities into a concrete, combinable instrument control vocabulary, surface high-leverage unexposed features, and bridge high-level instrument vision with per-capability design depth.
novelty: Treats embedding capabilities as a unified, orthogonal set of frame knobs—most notably pairing MRL resolution zoom with cluster-scale rungs as a 2-D zoom and deriving context-as-pole and context-strain from isolated-vs-in-context vector differences.
contribution: A structured mapping of pplx embedding capabilities to instrument affordances, a synthesized frame-chooser vocabulary, and a set of extension candidates (MRL zoom, binary full-corpus view, embedder loadout, context-as-pole/strain) that a fused system could keep as design options.
summary_for_embedding: A design document mapping pplx embedding-model capabilities to dual-faced instrument affordances and synthesizing them into a single frame-chooser control vocabulary.
intention_for_embedding: To identify how each pplx capability can become a combinable reading frame and which extensions are most worth implementing in the instrument.
